knownIssues.title: Requires two DP 1.4 outputs — limits GPU compatibility
knownIssues.description: The 8KX uses two separate DisplayPort 1.4 connections (one per eye panel) and requires a GPU with at least two DP 1.4 outputs. Multi-monitor users may run out of outputs.
knownIssues.workaround: Verify your GPU has two available DP 1.4 ports. Disconnect secondary monitors during VR if you have port conflicts. HDMI outputs cannot substitute.

knownIssues.title: PiTool / Pimax Play software requires careful version management
knownIssues.description: The Pimax software ecosystem has historically had significant stability issues. Updates have caused regressions. The 8KX is an older product and may not receive timely fixes.
knownIssues.workaround: Check the Pimax community forums for stable PiTool version recommendations. Keep a backup of known-working software before updating. Run PiTool as administrator.

knownIssues.title: Wide FOV causes edge distortion in some games
knownIssues.description: The 8KX's 170° FOV requires geometry correction that some games do not implement correctly, resulting in visible warping in the peripheral vision.
knownIssues.workaround: Use Pimax's 'Normal' or 'Large' FOV mode instead of 'Large+' to reduce distortion at the cost of some peripheral FOV. Per-game FOV profiles can be set in PiTool.

optimizationTips.tip: Use 75Hz at Normal FOV for the best performance-to-quality ratio
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Running at 75Hz with a slightly narrower field of view makes the 8KX much more manageable for your GPU without losing much quality.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: 3840x2160 per eye at 90Hz requires extraordinary GPU resources. Dropping to 75Hz reduces the frame time budget pressure by 17% and is imperceptible to most users. Normal FOV mode also reduces the render target area, saving an additional 15-20% GPU cost compared to Large+ FOV.

optimizationTips.tip: Do not supersample — native resolution is the render target
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The 8KX is already at the limit of what GPUs can render. Never turn supersampling above 100%.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: 3840x2160 per eye is demanding enough that rendering at 100% SteamVR SS already pushes top-tier GPUs. Above 100% SS reduces framerates dramatically with no visible clarity improvement — the panel resolves no finer detail than native. Focus GPU budget on stable framerates.

optimizationTips.tip: Enable Smart Smoothing at the 45fps → 90fps ratio
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Pimax's frame smoothing feature makes the headset tolerable even when your GPU can't keep up with the demanding resolution.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: At 3840x2160 per eye, even RTX 3090-class GPUs struggle with demanding titles at 90Hz. Smart Smoothing at 45fps→90Hz is often the only way to achieve acceptable comfort. Enable in PiTool per-application. Combined with Normal FOV, this makes more titles playable.
